perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Information ” means: [(a) any information disclosed b y [or on behalf of] the Disclose r to the Recipient [during the Term / before the end of the Term] (whether disclosed in writing, orally or otherwise) that at the time of disclosure: (i) was marked as “confidential”; or (ii) should have been reasonably understood by the Recipient to be confidential;] [(b) the terms [(but not the existence)] of this Agreement; and]5 [(c) [specify other confidential information here ];] “Permitted Purpose ” means input with respect to Purpose being input with respect to input with respect to Urban Wind Turbines “ TM “ and “Term ” means the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Information ” means: [(a) any information disclosed b y [or on behalf of] the Disclose r to the Recipient [during the Term / before the end of the Term] (whether disclosed in writing, orally or otherwise) that at the time of disclosure: (i) was marked as “confidential”; or (ii) should have been reasonably understood by the Recipient to be confidential;] [(b) the terms [(but not the existence)] of this Agreement; and]5 [(c) [specify other confidential information here ];] “Permitted Purpose ” means any activity related to Urban Wind Turbines “ TM “, irrespective of whether it directly involves input or development. and “Term ” means the duration of 1 year from the effective date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Permitted Purpose' has been broadened to include 'any activity related to Urban Wind Turbines', contradicting the more restrictive definition of 'input with respect to Purpose being input with respect to input with respect to Urban Wind Turbines “ TM “'. Additionally, the term is now defined to be 1 year from the effective date, but it is stated that it can continue indefinitely.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agr eement will come into force on the [date of its execution]6 and will continue in 1 This is a standard template non disclosure agreement (sometimes called a “confidentiality agreement”). The template is suitable for use whe re one party is disclosing confidential information – for example, for the purpose of exploring whether to enter into partnership, a joint venture or another business arrangement or contract. 2 The date should be the date of signature; if the parties sign on different dates, it should be the date of the last signature. 3 Where a party to the contract is a sole trader or partnership rather than a company, the following party definitions may be used: sole trader: “[ INDIVIDUAL NAME ] trading as [ business n ame], which has its principal place of business at [address ] (the “[ Disclose r/Recipient ]”)” partnership: “[ PARTNERSHIP NAME ], a partnership established under [English] law having its principal place of business at [ address ] (the “[ Disclose r/Recipient ]”)” 4 You may wish to c hange the defined terms Disclose r and Recipient to suit the circumstances of your agreement. 5 It may be that only some of the terms are confidential. 6 Where Confidential Information has been disclosed before the date of execution ( i.e. signature by both parties) then you should consider making the agreement retrospective, and specifying here that the agreement came into force on a particular date before the date of execution. force [indefinitely, unless and until terminated in accordance with Clause [5] / until [ date] [event ], upon which it will terminate automatically, unless terminated in accorda nce with Clause [5]].
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'Permitted Purpose' and introduces a fixed term of '1 year', contradicting the original agreement's clause stating the agreement continues 'indefinitely'. This directly creates an inconsistency regarding the duration and scope of the agreement's terms, therefore the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: 3.  Confidentiality obligations 3.1 The Recipient agrees and undertakes: (a) that it will keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential and will not disclose any part of it to any o ther person without the Disclose r's prior written consent[, and then only under conditions of confidentiality no less onerous than those contained in this Agreement]; (b) that it will use the same degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as it uses to protect its own confidential i nformation of a similar nature, being at least a reasonable degree of care; and [(c) that it will act in good faith at all times in relation to the Confidential Information, and will not use any of the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Permitted Purpose.]
perturbation.changed_text: 3.  Confidentiality obligations 3.1 The Recipient agrees and undertakes: (a) that it will keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential and will not disclose any part of it to any o ther person without the Disclose r's prior written consent[, and then only under conditions of confidentiality no less onerous than those contained in this Agreement]; (b) that it will use commercially reasonable efforts to protect the Confidential Information; and [(c) that it will act in good faith at all times in relation to the Confidential Information, and may use the Confidential Information for purposes beyond the Permitted Purpose, provided it benefits the Recipient .]
perturbation.explanation: The change in 3.1(b) replaces 'the same degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, being at least a reasonable degree of care' with 'commercially reasonable efforts to protect the Confidential Information.' This lowers the standard of care. The change in 3.1(c) adds that the Recipient may use the Confidential Information for purposes beyond the Permitted Purpose, provided it benefits the Recipient, is in contradiction with 3.1(a) that mentions information strictly confidential and it will not disclose any part of it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 The Recipient agrees and undertakes: (a) that it will keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential and will not disclose any part of it to any o ther person without the Disclose r's prior written consent[, and then only under conditions of confidentiality no less onerous than those contained in this Agreement]; (b) that it will use commercially reasonable efforts to protect the Confidential Information; and [(c) that it will act in good faith at all times in relation to the Confidential Information, and may use the Confidential Information for purposes beyond the Permitted Purpose, provided it benefits the Recipient .]
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text mandates keeping Confidential Information strictly confidential and not disclosing it without prior written consent. The changed text now allows the Recipient to use the Confidential Information for purposes beyond the Permitted Purpose, provided it benefits the Recipient, which directly contradicts the confidentiality and non-disclosure requirements outlined previously. The Contradicted text is the same as the changed text because the change is creating a contradiction to another section of the original text.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Notwithstanding Clause [3.1(a)], the Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to its [officers, employees, professional advisers, insurers, agents and sub -contractors] [who have a need to have access to the Confidenti al Information for the performance of their work with respect to the Permitted Purpose and] who are bound by a written agreement or professional obligation to protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Notwithstanding Clause [3.1(a)], the Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to its [officers, employees, professional advisers, insurers, agents and sub -contractors] without any requirement that they be bound by a written agreement or professional obligation to protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirement that the recipients of Confidential Information be bound by confidentiality agreements directly contradicts the intent of Clause 3.1(a), which mandates strict confidentiality and creates uncertainty regarding the overall protection of the disclosed information. This undermines the Discloser's expectation of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 The Recipient agrees and undertakes: (a) that it will keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential and will not disclose any part of it to any o ther person without the Disclose r's prior written consent[, and then only under conditions of confidentiality no less onerous than those contained in this Agreement]; (b) that it will use commercially reasonable efforts to protect the Confidential Information; and [(c) that it will act in good faith at all times in relation to the Confidential Information, and may use the Confidential Information for purposes beyond the Permitted Purpose, provided it benefits the Recipient .]
perturbation.justification: The original text of Clause 3.2 allowed the Recipient to disclose Confidential Information to its officers, employees, etc., only if they were bound by confidentiality agreements. The changed text removes this requirement. Clause 3.1(a) states that the Recipient must keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential and only disclose it under conditions of confidentiality no less onerous than those in the Agreement. By removing the requirement for confidentiality agreements in Clause 3.2, the changed text directly contradicts Clause 3.1(a), creating an in-text contradiction. The change breaks the compatibility between the sections, as it allows for disclosure without the necessary confidentiality protections outlined in Clause 3.1(a).

perturbation.original_text: 5.  Termination 5.1 [Either party] may termina te this Agreement [forthwith] at any time by giving written notice of termination to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.  Termination 5.1 The Discloser may terminate this Agreement [forthwith] at any time by giving written notice of termination to the other party. The Recipient can only terminate this agreement if the Discloser is in breach.
perturbation.explanation: By restricting the Recipient's ability to terminate the agreement only to instances where the Discloser is in breach, while allowing the Discloser to terminate at any time, creates a conflict. The original clause gives both parties equal termination rights, while the altered clause creates an imbalance. The right to terminate 'at any time' is no longer applicable to both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.  Termination 5.1 The Discloser may terminate this Agreement [forthwith] at any time by giving written notice of termination to the other party. The Recipient can only terminate this agreement if the Discloser is in breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 5.1 to restrict the Recipient's ability to terminate the agreement, allowing termination only if the Discloser is in breach, while the Discloser can terminate at any time. This directly contradicts the implied equal termination rights suggested by the original clause, which states either party may terminate. Because the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, and the change restricts one parties ability to terminate at will, which would be assumed as both parties originally, this means there is a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: [(a) the Recipient will immediately cease to use the Confidential Information; and] (b) all the provisions of this Agreement will cease to have effect, save that the following provisions of this Agreement will survive and continue to have effect (in accordance with their terms or otherwise indefinitely): Clauses [1, 3, 5.2 to 5.4, and 6]. [5.3 Within [5] working days following the date of effective termination of this Agreement the Recipient will d estroy or return to the Disclose r (at the Disclose r's option) all media containing Confidential Information, and will irrevocably delete and remove all Confidential Information from its co mputer systems.]
perturbation.changed_text: [(a) the Recipient will immediately cease to use the Confidential Information, unless otherwise agreed upon by the Discloser; and] (b) all the provisions of this Agreement will cease to have effect, save that the following provisions of this Agreement will survive and continue to have effect (in accordance with their terms or otherwise indefinitely): Clauses [1, 3, 5.2 to 5.4, and 6]. [5.3 Within [30] working days following the date of effective termination of this Agreement the Recipient will d estroy or return to the Disclose r (at the Disclose r's option) all media containing Confidential Information, and will irrevocably delete and remove all Confidential Information from its co mputer systems.]
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'unless otherwise agreed upon by the Discloser' to clause 5.2(a) contradicts the original intent of immediate cessation of use. The increase in the timeframe from 5 to 30 days for returning or destroying the Confidential Information creates further discrepancy, especially since section 5.2 says that it will cease to have effect. This creates ambiguity around how long the obligations exist. Also it contradicts the 'immediately cease to use the Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: [(a) the Recipient will immediately cease to use the Confidential Information, unless otherwise agreed upon by the Discloser; and] (b) all the provisions of this Agreement will cease to have effect, save that the following provisions of this Agreement will survive and continue to have effect (in accordance with their terms or otherwise indefinitely): Clauses [1, 3, 5.2 to 5.4, and 6]. [5.3 Within [30] working days following the date of effective termination of this Agreement the Recipient will d estroy or return to the Disclose r (at the Disclose r's option) all media containing Confidential Information, and will irrevocably delete and remove all Confidential Information from its co mputer systems.]
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'unless otherwise agreed upon by the Discloser' in the changed text directly contradicts the initial requirement of immediate cessation of use of confidential information, as stated in the original text and contradicted text, and further made ambiguous by the extension to 30 days. This conflicts with the section's survival clause. The changes create ambiguity and inconsistency, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
